title: Release Preparation
description: Prepare the next ZAgenticLoop npm release using package-level tags and Roadmap-Sliced handoff discipline.

1-1.label: Release scope and version policy

decisions.q: Which packages have release-relevant local changes since their latest package tag?
decisions.answer: Release candidates: @jununfly/zj-loop-core, @jununfly/zj-loop-init, @jununfly/zj-loop-cost, @jununfly/zj-loop-audit, @jununfly/zj-loop-sync, @jununfly/zj-loop-mcp-server. @jununfly/zj-goal-audit has no package-local changes since zj-goal-audit-v0.1.1.
decisions.note: Verified by git diff --name-only <latest-package-tag>..HEAD per package and npm view latest versions.

decisions.q: Should the release include every package with package-local changes and skip unchanged packages?
decisions.answer: Yes. Publish patch releases for core, init, cost, audit, sync, and mcp-server; skip goal-audit because it has no package-local change since its latest tag.
decisions.note: User confirmed. README must also be updated to emphasize the two completed live routes and summarize major architecture documents.

1-1.notes: Scope confirmed: release six changed packages as patch versions; skip zj-goal-audit.

1-2.label: Package version bump and changelog readiness

decisions.q: How should package versions be bumped for this release?
decisions.answer: Patch bump the six changed release-managed packages: core 0.1.4, init 0.1.7, cost 0.1.5, audit 0.1.4, sync 0.1.3, mcp-server 0.1.4. Leave goal-audit at 0.1.1.
decisions.note: Package lock root versions were updated by npm version --no-git-tag-version. README now presents the two dogfood-proven routes and links major architecture docs.

1-2.notes: Versions and README release positioning updated. Audit changelog has 0.1.4 entry; other changed packages do not currently maintain package-local changelogs.

1-3.label: Release verification gates

decisions.q: Which release verification gates passed before PR handoff?
decisions.answer: Passed: six changed package test suites; npm run test:release-workflows; npm run test:release-ready; npm run test:generated-bundle-release-gate; npm run check:zj-loop-init; bash scripts/ci-audit-gates.sh; bash scripts/ci-validate-gates.sh; git diff --check.
decisions.note: ci-validate first hit npm registry DNS in sandbox, then passed with network access. Generated bundle gate exposed stale core pins; fixed templates, generated workflows, docs, and tests.

1-3.notes: All release verification gates passed after updating generated workflow/package pins and test expectations.

1-4.label: Post-publish smoke and closeout
1-4.notes: After publish, smoke npm view/npx for released packages and then close out process roadmap into durable release notes if needed.

1-5.label: Tag push and workflow monitoring

decisions.q: When should release tags be pushed?
decisions.answer: After the release-prep PR is reviewed and merged to origin/main. Tags should be created from the final main commit, not from the preparation branch.
decisions.note: This preserves human review for workflow/template changes and keeps tag-triggered npm publish workflows on reviewed main.

1-5.notes: Pending human merge of release-prep PR. After merge, tag six package releases from origin/main and monitor release workflows.
